message:
feat(explain): propagate explanation field through trace and history

- Add explanation field to MatchInfo in trace output
- Display explanation with fallback in explain traces
- Add rule_id field to history schema (v4 migration)
- Add helper methods for computing rule_id from pack_id:pattern_name
- Update test signatures for explanation parameter

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -54,7 +54,7 @@
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3","title":"Simulate: output formats (pretty/json) + redaction/truncation","description":"## Objective

Implement `dcg simulate` output formatting in both human and machine forms.

## Pretty output requirements

- Summary header (totals + elapsed time)
- Top rules table:
  - rule_id
  - count
  - decision (deny/warn)
  - 1-line reason (if available)
- Optional section: sample commands per top rule (truncated + redacted)

## JSON output requirements

- `schema_version`
- totals
- `rules[]`: `{ rule_id, pack_id, decision, count, reason?, examples[] }`
- `errors[]`: parse errors counts, malformed lines count

## Privacy

- Default to truncation in examples.
- Support `--redact` consistent with scan/explain.

## Acceptance criteria

- Output is stable for golden tests.
- JSON output is valid and additive-only for schema v1.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.8.2` (aggregation)
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### Determinism
- Output formats must be deterministic and stable for diffing:
  - stable JSON schema versioning
  - stable sorting of aggregated tables
  - stable truncation/redaction rules","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:16:19.160687587-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T00:12:36.461516365-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T00:12:36.461516365-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented pretty/JSON output formats with redaction/truncation. Added --redact, --truncate, --top CLI flags. Created SimulateJsonOutput with errors object. All 22 simulate tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:16:29.475130683-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:16:34.556618726-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.4","title":"Simulate: fixtures + integration tests + detailed logging","description":"## Objective

Add tests that make `dcg simulate` trustworthy and easy to evolve.

## Test strategy

### Unit tests

- parser unit tests (format detection) live with `git_safety_guard-1gt.8.1`.

### Integration tests (this task)

- Run the compiled binary with `dcg simulate` against fixture log files.
- Assert:
  - exit codes
  - key summary counts
  - top rule ordering stability
  - redaction/truncation behavior

## Logging requirements

Test failures should print:

- which fixture file
- which line number caused parse failure
- the offending line (truncated)
- the expected vs actual summary

## Acceptance criteria

- Tests are deterministic across runs.
- Adding a new fixture regression is easy.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3` (output)

Commit 080db43.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:17:02.125735949-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:17:07.167409749-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","title":"[PACK] remote.ssh - SSH Pack","description":"# SSH Remote Access Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for SSH operations, particularly remote command execution.

## Destructive Patterns
### Remote Command Execution
- `ssh host 'dangerous-command'` - Detect destructive commands executed remotely
- Piping destructive scripts through SSH
- Redirecting scripts into SSH

### Key Management
- `ssh-keygen -R host` - Removes known hosts (security implication)
- Deleting SSH keys

### Config Operations
- Modifying ~/.ssh/config destructively
- Removing authorized_keys entries

## Safe Patterns
- `ssh -V` (version)
- `ssh-keygen -l` (fingerprint display)
- `ssh-keyscan` (key scanning)
- `ssh host` (interactive session - user takes responsibility)
- `ssh host 'safe-command'` (non-destructive remote commands)

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: remote.* (git_safety_guard-g5o)
- Keywords: ssh, ssh-keygen
- CRITICAL: Must detect when OTHER destructive commands are tunneled through SSH

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for remote delete commands via SSH
- [ ] Unit tests for piped script detection
- [ ] Unit tests for key management operations
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/remote_ssh.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Remote destructive commands detected
- [ ] Key removal operations blocked
- [ ] Safe SSH usage allowed
- [ ] Interactive sessions not blocked","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:11:13.436955387-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T03:00:07.922675119-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T03:00:07.922675119-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented remote.ssh pack with 6 destructive patterns and 10 safe patterns; all 8 tests pass","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.132964887-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.177154661-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.223350479-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.270713954-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.316989212-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:44.363582267-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.365141001-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gv","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:13.41138624-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-1n85","title":"[Explain] Write explanations for core.filesystem patterns","description":"# Task: Write explanations for core.filesystem patterns

## Parent Epic: Visual Explanations (git_safety_guard-r97e)

## Objective

Write detailed, helpful explanations for all destructive patterns in the core.filesystem pack. These explanations will be shown to users when commands are blocked.

## Patterns to Document

Review src/packs/core/filesystem.rs for all destructive patterns. Expected patterns include:
- Recursive deletion of root/home paths
- chmod 777 (world-writable permissions)  
- chown to root
- dd operations on block devices
- Filesystem format commands

## Explanation Template

For each pattern, provide:

1. WHAT: One sentence describing the destructive action
2. WHY DANGEROUS: What could go wrong (data loss, security risk, etc.)
3. ALTERNATIVE: Safer way to accomplish the goal
4. PREVIEW: Command to check before executing (if applicable)

## Example Explanation

Pattern: recursive-delete-root

Explanation: |
  This command would recursively delete files starting from the root
  or home directory. This is almost always catastrophic - it will
  destroy your operating system, all applications, and all user data.
  There is no recovery without backups.
  
  If you need to delete specific files, use explicit paths like:
    find /specific/path -name "pattern" -delete
  
  Always preview first with:
    find /specific/path -name "pattern" -print

## File Location

src/packs/core/filesystem.rs

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every destructive pattern in filesystem pack has explanation
- Explanations are concise but complete (2-4 sentences)
- At least one alternative suggested per pattern
- Preview commands where applicable

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1rgy","title":"avoid panics in production paths (unwrap/unreachable)","description":"Replace unwrap/unreachable in non-test paths (CLI allowlist I/O, scan extractor, allowlist parsing, context classifier) with error handling or safe fallbacks to avoid panics on malformed input or I/O failures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-12T17:50:54.93986901-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T17:54:17.468726749-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T17:54:17.468726749-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1s6d","title":"Fix shell continuation handling when backslash is in comment","description":"Shell extractor treats trailing backslash inside an inline comment as a line continuation, which can join the next line and hide destructive commands. Strip inline comments before continuation detection and add regression test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-12T12:10:45.900938947-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T12:13:04.691630897-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T12:13:04.691630897-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1uf","title":"E2E: path normalization + quoting/encoding matrix","description":"## Objective

Expand E2E coverage around tricky path/quoting scenarios that historically cause false positives/negatives.

## Scope

- Absolute paths (`/usr/bin/git`, `/bin/rm`) plus quoted command words.
- Mixed quoting, escaped characters, Unicode edge cases, env assignments, and sudo/env wrappers.
- Encode tests for command substitution and pipeline contexts.

## Acceptance criteria

- New matrix cases added to E2E suite with clear expected results.
- Failures are easy to diagnose from logs.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-08T23:31:06.365458279-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-09T04:20:14.963723778-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-09T04:20:14.963723778-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: added hermetic hook-mode E2E matrix for path/quoting/wrapper cases; fixed pack-aware quick reject to normalize before span gating; fixed hook budget + heredoc pipeline budget mismatch so inline-script/heredoc blocking works in hook mode; hardened e2e script to be hermetic and avoid destructive cleanup.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1uf","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-7e0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-08T23:32:51.636130211-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -15,6 +15,20 @@ Use this checklist when adding a new pack to `destructive_command_guard`.
 - [ ] Implement `destructive_patterns` (regex + reason).
 - [ ] Implement `safe_patterns` (if needed for whitelist).
 
+## 2a. Explanation Style Guide (for destructive patterns)
+
+Explanations are optional but recommended for user-facing clarity. When present:
+
+- Keep it short: 2-4 sentences, ~400 chars or less.
+- Stay neutral and factual; avoid scaremongering or moralizing.
+- Structure:
+  1) What the command does.
+  2) Why that is risky (data loss, downtime, security blast radius).
+  3) Safer alternative and/or preview command.
+- Use concrete nouns and commands (e.g., `git reset --soft`, `--dry-run`).
+- Avoid absolutes unless accurate (prefer "often" or "can").
+- Match the existing `reason` but add real detail (not repetition).
+
 ## 3. Unit Testing
 - [ ] Copy `src/packs/test_template.rs` content to `src/packs/<category>/<tool>.rs` (mod tests).
 - [ ] Update `test_pack_creation` to use `validate_pack`.
